components.name: Accordion/Default

props.name: children
props.description: The content displayed within the accordion panel

props.name: title
props.description: The header text displayed in the accordion trigger button

props.name: expanded
props.description: Controls whether the accordion panel is expanded or collapsed

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the accordion, preventing expansion or collapse

props.name: onChange
props.description: Callback fired when the accordion expanded state changes

props.name: defaultExpanded
props.description: The initial expanded state when uncontrolled

components.usage: Use accordions to organize related content into collapsible sections, allowing users to show and hide information on demand. Ideal for FAQs, settings panels, or any content where users only need to view a subset at a time. Accordions reduce cognitive load by progressively disclosing information.
components.never: Never nest accordions within accordions as this creates confusing hierarchies. Never use accordions for critical information that users must see immediately. Never disable individual accordion items without clear visual indication and explanation.
components.compose: Wrap multiple accordion items within an AccordionGroup or parent container to manage expanded state behavior (single vs multiple expansion). Pair with icons to indicate expand/collapse state and provide visual affordances.

aria.role: button for trigger with aria-expanded, region for panel content
aria.keyboard: Enter or Space to toggle expansion, Tab to move between accordion headers, focus management to panel content when expanded
aria.screenReader: Announces accordion header text, expanded or collapsed state, and number of items if in a group. Panel content is read when expanded.

components.name: Avatar

props.name: variant
props.description: Size variant of the avatar

props.name: size
props.description: Explicit size of the avatar

props.name: src
props.description: Image source URL for the avatar

props.name: alt
props.description: Alternative text describing the person or entity

props.name: initials
props.description: Fallback initials when no image is provided

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables interactive functionality when Avatar is clickable

props.name: onClick
props.description: Click handler for interactive avatars

props.name: children
props.description: Custom content rendered inside the avatar

components.usage: Use Avatar to represent a user or entity with an image, initials, or icon. Choose a size that matches the visual hierarchy and context—XS for compact lists, MD for standard user profiles, XL for profile headers. Avatars should always have accessible labels for screen readers.
components.never: Never use Avatar for decorative images unrelated to user identity. Never omit alternative text or accessible labeling. Never use sizes smaller than the minimum touch target (44x44px) when the Avatar is interactive.
components.compose: Wrap Avatar in a button or link when it triggers navigation or actions. Pair with text labels for user names in lists or headers to provide context beyond the visual representation.

aria.keyboard: When interactive, responds to Enter and Space keys to activate. Focus indicator must be visible.
aria.screenReader: Announces the alt text or aria-label describing the user or entity. Interactive avatars announce as buttons with the person's name.

components.name: Button

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual style variant that conveys action hierarchy and intent

props.name: size
props.description: Controls the button dimensions and internal spacing

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables button interaction and applies disabled styling

props.name: onClick
props.description: Callback fired when the button is clicked

props.name: children
props.description: Button label text or content

components.usage: Use buttons to trigger actions, submit forms, or navigate to new pages. Primary buttons should represent the main action on a page, with only one primary per section. Neutral and Ghost variants are for secondary actions, while Danger is reserved for destructive operations like delete or remove.
components.never: Never use a button for navigation alone—use a link instead. Never stack multiple Primary buttons in the same context as this creates competing hierarchy. Never disable a button without providing clear feedback about why it's disabled or how to enable it.
components.compose: Buttons can be grouped in button groups or toolbars for related actions. Wrap buttons in form elements when submitting data, or use within modal footers for confirmation actions.

aria.keyboard: Activates with Space or Enter keys. Tab to focus, Shift+Tab to reverse focus.
aria.screenReader: Announces button label, role, and disabled state when present

components.name: Input

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual variant that communicates the input's current state

props.name: disabled
props.description: Disables the input and prevents user interaction

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled value of the input field

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Placeholder text displayed when input is empty

props.name: type
props.description: HTML input type that determines input behavior and validation

props.name: onChange
props.description: Callback fired when the input value changes

props.name: onFocus
props.description: Callback fired when the input receives focus

props.name: onBlur
props.description: Callback fired when the input loses focus

props.name: error
props.description: When true, applies error styling and indicates validation failure

props.name: required
props.description: Marks the input as required for form submission

components.usage: Use Input for single-line text entry in forms where users need to provide short textual information like names, emails, or search queries. Inputs should always have a clear label and optionally helper text to guide users on expected format or requirements. Use the appropriate variant to communicate state (focus, error, disabled) to users.
components.never: Never use Input for multi-line text entry — use Textarea instead. Never omit labels or rely solely on placeholder text for field identification, as placeholders disappear on input and create accessibility barriers. Never use disabled state for read-only data that users need to copy — use read-only styling or plain text instead.
components.compose: Wrap Input with a label element or use a Field wrapper component that handles label, helper text, and error message layout. Pair with validation feedback messages positioned below the input and associate them using aria-describedby.

aria.keyboard: Standard text input interactions: Type to enter text, Tab to navigate between fields, Enter to submit parent form. Arrow keys move cursor within text.
aria.screenReader: Screen readers announce the label, current value, required state, and any error or helper text associated via aria-describedby. Error state is announced when aria-invalid is true.
